title: Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum
what_description: Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orchidaceae. It is a terrestrial orchid with a stemless habit, 10-30 cm tall, and a basal rosette of 2-4 leaves, 3-8 cm long, lance-shaped, and dark green. The inflorescence is a spike of 5-15 flowers, 3-4 cm long, with sepals and petals fused into a labellum, 1.5-2.5 cm long, and a greenish-brown lip, 1.5-2.5 cm long. The flowers are typically purple with a yellow or orange lip and are pollinated by insects. It is endemic to the Balkan Peninsula, specifically in Albania, Kosovo, and North Macedonia.
ailments_description: The most common ailments you can heal with Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum are erectile dysfunction due to its high content of icariin, a flavonoid that inhibits PDE5, an enzyme responsible for the condition. Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum also has anti-inflammatory properties, which help alleviate symptoms associated with inflammation and conditions like arthritis and asthma, where inflammation plays a key role. Impotence is another condition that can be treated with Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum due to its ability to relax the smooth muscles in the corpus cavernosum. Diabetes and hypertension can be managed with Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum, which has been shown to reduce blood glucose levels and lower blood pressure due to its flavonoid and phenolic acid content. Additionally, Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum has been used to treat urinary tract infections, prostate issues, kidney stones, depression, cardiovascular disease, prostatitis, eczema, incontinence, insomnia, cough, anxiety, and infertility due to its antioxidant and immunomodulatory properties.
properties_description: The main therapeutic properties of Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum are its antioxidant activity, which is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids that can neutralize free radicals and protect against oxidative stress. The anti-inflammatory properties of this plant are mediated by saponins and alkaloids, which can inhibit the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines and enzymes, thereby reducing inflammation and alleviating symptoms of conditions such as arthritis. Additionally, the antimicrobial properties of Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum are attributed to the presence of volatile oils and terpenoids, which can inhibit the growth of bacteria, fungi, and viruses, making it effective against various infections. The analgesic properties of this plant are due to the presence of alkaloids and glycosides, which can bind to opioid receptors and produce analgesic and anti-pain effects. Furthermore, the cardioprotective properties of Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum are attributed to the presence of flavonoids and saponins, which can improve cardiovascular function, reduce blood pressure, and prevent atherosclerosis.
constituents_description: The most important healing constituents of Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum are orchitin, a mucilage with demulcent properties, orchic acid, a compound with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, orchidin, a flavonoid glycoside with hepatoprotective and anti-inflammatory activities, glycosides, which exhibit anti-inflammatory and cardiotonic properties, and flavonoids, such as kaempferol and quercetin, which have antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial effects, contributing to the plant's use in traditional medicine for treating various ailments, including respiratory and gastrointestinal issues.
preparations_description: The main herbal preparations of Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum are infusion, tincture, decoction, tea and salve. Infusion is made by steeping the dried flowers and leaves in hot water to create a liquid extract used for treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Tincture is prepared by macerating the plant material in a solvent like ethanol to create a concentrated liquid used for treating conditions like inflammation and fever. Decoction is made by boiling the dried roots in water to create a liquid extract used for treating digestive issues such as constipation and diarrhea. Salve is prepared by infusing the plant material in a carrier oil to create a topical application used for treating skin conditions like eczema and wounds.
side_effects_description: The possible side effects of using Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum improperly include experiencing nausea due to its toxic compounds, such as protocerberide alkaloids, which can cause gastrointestinal disturbances. Suffering kidney damage is also a possibility due to the high concentration of these alkaloids, which can lead to acute renal failure if ingested in large quantities. Developing an allergic reaction or experiencing allergic reactions to Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum is also a risk, as some individuals may be sensitive to its compounds, such as orchinol, which can trigger an allergic response. This reaction can range from mild symptoms like hives and itching to severe anaphylaxis. The improper use of Orchis mascula subsp. scopulorum can lead to these side effects because of its toxic and allergenic properties.
